title: Smoky Paprika Glazed Irish Dates Stir-fry
description: A tangy and gooey date-night dish featuring smoked paprika for an added smokiness that complements the natural sweetness of Irish dates in a low-sodium-friendly recipe.

ingredients.name: 1 cup pitted Irish dates, soaked in water and drained
ingredients.notes: soak dates for at least an hour to reduce their natural sweetness

ingredients.name: 1 tsp smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: or regular paprika for a milder taste

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp honey or agave nectar (adjust to taste)
Fresh parsley or cilantro, finely chopped for garnish

instructions: Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the soaked Irish dates to the skillet and cook until they release their natural sweetness, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and smoked bacon if using.
Stir in the smoked paprika and honey or agave nectar, cooking for an additional 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Fold in chopped baby spinach leaves to wilt them gently with the glaze.
Remove from heat and garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro before serving.
